mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat, discount rates ease and equity valuations expand, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day returns. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation reduces the need for further tightening.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX returns are negative or below unconditional average in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS has a non-increasing 3-month trend, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent low marks weakening labor demand and higher recession risk, which depresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be clearest after a meaningful monthly deterioration rather than small noise.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not lower than the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad consumer and housing demand, which supports revenue growth and improves cyclical equity performance. The signal is positive only when both series advance together, because isolated strength in one sector does not confirm economy-wide demand acceleration.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition of 3-month gains in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if conditional 20-day SPX returns are not positive and above baseline in triggered joint-upturn windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and better cyclical capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should persist beyond short-lived monthly noise and is most reliable when labor conditions are stable.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods after the INDPRO moving-average breakout, or if the average return differential versus non-breakout periods is statistically indistinguishable.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is still low, real rates are likely to rise later and the market re-prices a future tightening path, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days. The mechanism depends on inflation pressure being the dominant new information, not on stable inflation.
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-triggered episodes do not show below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of episodes despite CPIAUCSL acceleration and low policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after a prior expansion signals broad demand deceleration, lower earnings growth, and weaker S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is negative because GDP weakness should transmit into revenues, margins, and risk appetite.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after negative GDPC1 growth readings, or if the sign hit rate is below 50% across all recession-signal quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE indicates a tight but still expanding labor market, which supports consumer income and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when payroll gains are accompanied by rising unemployment, because that combination points to unstable labor dynamics rather than healthy demand.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint-strength labor episodes fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond PAYEMS alone in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in HOUST after prior strength signals cooling housing demand and weaker cyclical sentiment, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is short-horizon because equity investors react quickly to housing-led growth surprises.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average after a prior peak
failure_condition: Rejected if 5-day forward SPX returns are not negative following housing rollover signals, or if the average post-trigger return is indistinguishable from zero across a full rolling year.
